answers: Black hole = region of space where gravity is so strong nothing can escape — not even light. Formed from massive star collapse.
Types: stellar (few solar masses), supermassive (millions-billions solar masses, center of galaxies), and primordial (theoretical).
Facts: time slows near black holes, first image captured 2019 (M87*), our galaxy has Sagittarius A* at its center.
Black holes don't suck everything in — they only pull objects that get close enough, just like any other gravity source.

feelings.question: black holes ki?
feelings.answer: Black holes are regions where gravity is so strong that nothing can escape. They form when massive stars collapse under their own gravitational force.
